parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations. Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI held by the covered entity or business associate.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with the general security requirements of the HIPAA Security Rule.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with the security policies and procedures of the covered entity or business associate.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures required by the HIPAA Security Rule for the covered entity or business associate.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of the workforce have appropriate access to ePHI and to prevent those workforce members who do not have authorized access from obtaining access to ePHI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures for the authorization and/or supervision of workforce members who work with ePHI or in locations where it might be accessed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures to determine that the access of a workforce member to ePHI is appropriate based on their role and responsibilities within the organization.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures for terminating access to ePHI when the employment of, or other arrangement with, a workforce member ends or as required by the access authorization policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to ePHI that are consistent with the applicable requirements of the HIPAA Privacy Rule.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If a health care clearinghouse is part of a larger organization, implement policies and procedures that protect the ePHI of the clearinghouse from unauthorized access by the larger organization.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures for granting access to ePHI, such as through access to a workstation, transaction, program, or process, based on the principle of least privilege.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures that, based upon the covered entity's or business associate's access authorization policies, establish, document, review, and modify a user's right of access to a workstation, transaction, program, or process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of the workforce, including management, covering recognition of threats, password management, and incident reporting procedures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide periodic security updates and reminders to the workforce regarding current threats, policy changes, and best practices for protecting ePHI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software such as viruses, worms, ransomware, and other threats to information systems containing ePHI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures for monitoring log-in attempts and reporting discrepancies, including failed authentication attempts, unusual access patterns, and access from unauthorized locations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures for creating, changing, and safeguarding passwords, including complexity requirements, expiration policies, and prohibition of password sharing.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents, including the identification, classification, and escalation of incidents that may affect the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of ePHI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents, mitigate to the extent practicable the harmful effects of known security incidents, and document security incidents and their outcomes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and implement policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence that damages systems containing ePHI, ensuring the continuity of critical business processes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of ePHI, including defining backup frequency, storage locations, and verification procedures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and implement procedures to restore any loss of data, including documented recovery time objectives, recovery point objectives, and procedures for system restoration.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and implement procedures to enable continuation of critical business processes for the protection of ePHI while operating in emergency mode during and after a crisis.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures for periodic testing and revision of contingency plans, including tabletop exercises, failover tests, and documented lessons learned.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Assess the relative criticality of specific applications and data in support of other contingency plan components, identifying systems essential for ePHI processing and their recovery priority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Perform a periodic technical and nontechnical evaluation, based initially upon the standards implemented under the Security Rule and subsequently in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of ePHI.

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts and Other Arrangements

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit ePHI on its behalf only if the covered entity obtains satisfactory assurances that the business associate will appropriately safeguard the information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Document the satisfactory assurances required by the business associate contract through a written contract or other arrangement that meets the applicable requirements of the HIPAA Rules.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to electronic information systems and the facilities in which they are housed, while ensuring that properly authorized access is allowed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and implement procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data under the disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operations plan in the event of an emergency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to safeguard the facility and the equipment therein from unauthorized physical access, tampering, and theft, including surveillance, locks, and visitor management.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures to control and validate a person's access to facilities based on their role or function, including visitor control and control of access to software programs for testing and revision.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to document repairs and modifications to the physical components of a facility which are related to security, such as hardware, walls, doors, and locks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings of a specific workstation or class of workstation that can access ePHI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access ePHI, to restrict access to authorized users, including cable locks, screen privacy filters, and secure placement.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain ePHI into and out of a facility, and the movement of these items within the facility.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of ePHI and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored, ensuring data is rendered unrecoverable through approved sanitization methods.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures for removal of ePHI from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use, using approved data wiping or degaussing techniques.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Maintain a record of the movements of hardware and electronic media and any person responsible therefor, including asset tracking and chain-of-custody documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Create a retrievable, exact copy of ePHI when needed before movement of equipment, ensuring that backed-up data is stored securely and can be restored if the original is lost or damaged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain ePHI to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights as specified in the access authorization policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity so that each user's activity can be individually audited and attributed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and implement procedures for obtaining necessary ePHI during an emergency, including break-glass procedures with appropriate logging and post-event review.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity to prevent unauthorized access to ePHI on unattended workstations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI at rest, using NIST-approved cryptographic algorithms such as AES-256 to render data unreadable to unauthorized individuals.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI, including access logs, authentication events, and data modification records.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction, ensuring that data remains accurate and complete throughout its lifecycle.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that ePHI has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner, such as digital signatures, checksums, or message authentication codes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed, using multi-factor authentication, biometrics, or other reliable identity verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to ePHI that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted ePHI is not improperly modified without detection, using message integrity checks such as TLS with HMAC.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement a mechanism to encrypt ePHI in transit whenever deemed appropriate, using TLS 1.2 or higher for all network transmissions containing protected health information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The contract between a covered entity and a business associate must meet the requirements of the HIPAA Security Rule, including provisions requiring the business associate to implement appropriate safeguards for ePHI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The contract must provide that the business associate will implement administrative, physical, and technical safeguards that reasonably and appropriately protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When a covered entity and its business associate are both governmental entities, the covered entity may comply through alternative arrangements such as a memorandum of understanding or other written agreement.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A group health plan must ensure that its plan sponsor will reasonably and appropriately safeguard ePHI created, received, maintained, or transmitted to or by the plan sponsor on behalf of the group health plan.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The plan documents of the group health plan must be amended to incorporate provisions requiring the plan sponsor to implement reasonable and appropriate safeguards to protect ePHI and to report security incidents to the group health plan.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement reasonable and appropriate policies and procedures to comply with the standards, implementation specifications, and other requirements of the HIPAA Security Rule, taking into account the size, complexity, and capabilities of the covered entity.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Retain required documentation for six years from the date of its creation or the date when it last was in effect, whichever is later.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Make documentation available to those persons responsible for implementing the procedures to which the documentation pertains, ensuring authorized personnel can access current policies at all times.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Review documentation periodically and update as needed in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of ePHI, including changes in technology, regulations, or threats.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Document actions, activities, or assessments required by the HIPAA Security Rule, maintaining written or electronic records of compliance activities and their outcomes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Retain all required documentation, including policies, procedures, and records of actions and assessments, for the mandated retention period and in a format that is accessible and retrievable.
